document.title: a-Si:H/CsI(Tl) flat-panel versus computed radiography for chest imaging applications: image quality metrics measurement
productModels: Fuji Medical Systems USA FCR AC-3
authorityOrJurisdiction: American Association of Physicists in Medicine

sourceSummary.oneLine: Medical Physics article measuring and comparing MTF, NPS, NEQ, and DQE for a commercial a-Si:H/CsI(Tl) flat-panel digital chest system and a commercial computed radiography system.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Literature evidence for image quality metrics of flat-panel and computed radiography systems under clinical imaging conditions; not an FDA regulatory decision document.
keyPoints: The study compared a commercial amorphous silicon/cesium iodide flat-panel based digital chest system with a commercial CR system under clinical imaging conditions.
The flat-panel system had slightly lower MTF but significantly higher DQE than the CR system.
The flat-panel DQE increased with exposure while CR DQE decreased slightly with exposure.
The FP detector studied was GE Revolution XQ/i; the CR system was Fuji FCR AC-3 with ST-VN imaging plate.
Beam qualities were compared with IEC standard RQA5 and RQA9 conditions.

evidence.claim: The article was published in Medical Physics, volume 31, page 98, with DOI 10.1118/1.1625102.
The article was accepted for publication on 5 September 2003 and published on 19 December 2003.
evidence.evidenceText: accepted for publication 5 September 2003; published 19 December 2003

evidence.claim: The study measured image quality metrics for commercial flat-panel and computed radiography systems under clinical imaging conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: we have measured the image properties for a commercial amorphous silicon/cesium iodide [a-Si:H/CsI(Tl)] flat-panel based digital chest system and a commercial CR system under clinical imaging conditions

evidence.claim: The measured image quality metrics included MTF, NPS, and DQE.
evidence.evidenceText: measurements of image quality metrics, including the modulation transfer functions (MTFs), noise power spectra (NPSs), and detective quantum efficiencies (DQEs)

evidence.claim: The flat-panel system had slightly lower MTF but significantly higher DQE than the CR system.
evidence.evidenceText: The results show that the flat-panel system has slightly lower MTF but significantly higher DQEs than the CR system.

evidence.claim: The flat-panel DQE increased with exposure while the CR DQE decreased slightly with exposure.
evidence.evidenceText: The DQEs of the flat-panel system were found to increase with the exposure while those of the CR system decrease slightly with the exposure.

evidence.claim: The flat-panel detector system used a 40.4 cm by 40.4 cm active area and 200 micrometer pixel pitch.
evidence.evidenceText: active area of 40.4 cm340.4 cm (15.9 in.315.9 in.) and a pixel pitch of 200 mm

evidence.claim: The flat-panel detector was part of the GE Revolution XQ/i digital chest unit.
evidence.evidenceText: manufactured as part of a stand-alone digital chest unit (Revolution XQ/i, GE Medical Systems, Milwaukee, WI)

evidence.claim: The CR system was a Fuji FCR AC-3 image reader used with an ST-VN imaging plate.
evidence.evidenceText: The CR system studied and compared in this paper was a FCR AC-3 CR image reader used in conjunction with a standard imaging plate ST-VN (Fuji Medical Systems USA, Inc., Stamford, CT).

evidence.claim: The study used 70 kVp and 120 kVp exposures with a 0.5 mm copper plate.
evidence.evidenceText: Exposures were made at 70 kVP and 120 kVP... A 0.5-mm thick copper plate was added to simulate attenuation and filtration by the patient.

evidence.claim: The study compared beam qualities to IEC standard RQA5 and RQA9 conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: close to those specified in the IEC standard (Table II): 70 kVp with 7.1 or 6.9 mm Al compared to 7.1 mm Al for RQA5 and 120 kVp with 10.6 or 10.4 mm Al compared to 11.5 mm Al for RQA9.

evidence.claim: The paper concludes that FP NPS values were generally lower than CR NPS values and decreased faster with spatial frequency.
evidence.evidenceText: The NPSs of the FP system were found to be generally lower than those of the CR system and decrease at a faster rate with the spatial frequency.
